message:
feat(telegram): add singleton-writer lease fencing and polling cursor state persistence

Implement singleton-writer lease fencing and polling offset persistence
for the Telegram connector:

- TelegramPollLease with acquire/renew/release via atomic JSON files
- TelegramPollingCursorState for persisting update_id offset to disk
- Graceful shutdown with timeout before abort
- Handshake now requires zone_dir
- Polling loop renews lease on each iteration
- Tests: concurrent handshake fencing, malformed payload robustness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
